summary: Matched several previously stubbed gmvsmelee functions and tightened related game-mode, menu, and audio prototypes. The PR turns multiple `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations into typed signatures, implements CSS/SSS/match/sudden-death/results transition helpers, and clarifies KO star count handling, match-end flow, and audio mask setup for VS Melee scenes.
what_changed: Implemented previously unmatched gmvsmelee functions including `gm_801A5360`, `gm_801A5680`, `gm_801A57A8`, `gm_801A583C`, `gm_801A5C3C`, and `gm_801A5F64`.
Changed `gm_804D6730` from an extern scalar `u8` to `u8 gm_804D6730[6]`, and updated KO star count users to pass the array pointer directly instead of `&gm_804D6730`.
Typed `gm_801A5250` as returning `u8*` and documented it as returning KO star counts.

Added CSS and SSS scene transition logic: CSS exit copies `CSSData` back to `VsModeData` and configures audio masks; SSS exit either starts the game and configures stage-related audio or returns to a pending minor scene.

Added match entry setup that initializes `StartMeleeData` from `VsModeData`, copies rules and player init data, applies optional callbacks, calls `gm_801B0348`, checks rumble through `gm_8016F088`, and invokes `gm_80168FC4`.
Added sudden-death entry setup similar to match entry, but ending with `gm_801B0474(match_start_data, &gm_80479D98.match_end)`.

Implemented results-exit style logic in `gm_801A5F64`, including match-end processing, loser selection, pending minor-scene decisions, unlock/check calls, and cleanup calls to `lb_8001C550`, `lb_8001D164`, and `lb_8001CE00`.
Corrected assignment direction in `gm_801A5F00` so `gm_80479D98.match_end = *exit_info` after `gm_80177724(exit_info)`.

Updated many prototypes in `gm_1601.h` from unknown signatures to typed arguments and return values, especially around `MatchEnd`, `VsModeData`, `SSSData`, `StartMeleeData`, and game-mode helper functions.
Updated `gm_1B03.h` with `StartMeleeData*` and `MatchEnd*` signatures for `gm_801B0348` and `gm_801B0474`.
Typed audio helper prototypes `lbAudioAx_80026E84(s8)` and `lbAudioAx_80026EBC(u32)` as returning `u64`, matching their use as bitmasks passed to `lbAudioAx_8002702C`.
Changed `struct SSSData::start_game` from `bool` to `u8`, matching code that tests it as a byte flag.
smart_moves: The match work was paired with prototype cleanup across dependent headers, reducing reliance on `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` and making callsites more self-documenting.
The PR inferred `gm_804D6730` as a six-entry KO star count array, consistent with repeated loops over six player slots and CSS data storing `ko_star_counts`.
Audio setup was matched by recognizing that `lbAudioAx_80026E84` and `lbAudioAx_80026EBC` produce `u64` masks, which are accumulated or passed into `lbAudioAx_8002702C`.
The implementation preserved optional callback hooks for match and sudden-death setup, then copied all six `PlayerInitData` entries, matching the six-slot player model used elsewhere in the function.
The loser-selection helper `gm_801A5360` handles team and non-team standings differently, combining player and team small-loser fields for teams and asserting if no active loser candidate is found.

Scene-transition functions were named/commented in `gmvsmelee.h` with lifecycle roles such as `CSS_Enter`, `CSS_Exit`, `SSS_Enter`, `SSS_Exit`, `Match_Enter`, and tentative results/sudden-death labels, improving future navigation without overclaiming uncertain names.
decomp_lessons: When matching scene-manager glue, propagate type discoveries into all related headers immediately; typed signatures for `MatchEnd`, `VsModeData`, `StartMeleeData`, `SSSData`, and callback function pointers make downstream functions much easier to match.
Repeated loops over six player slots are strong evidence for array dimensions and can expose incorrect extern declarations such as scalar-versus-array globals.

Menu scene data often stores a full copy of mode data; matching enter/exit functions may require identifying whether the function copies from global mode state into scene-local data or from scene-local data back to global mode state.
Audio helper functions that feed mask arguments should be typed by their use at aggregation sites; here the functions needed `u64` returns because masks are ORed and passed to a `u64` audio API.
Use narrow field types when evidence supports them. `SSSData::start_game` became `u8` rather than `bool`, matching the structure layout and byte-flag access implied by the code.

For transition code with unlock/result checks, keep temporary labels conservative. The PR uses comments like `SuddenDeath_Enter ??` and `Results_Exit ??`, which is preferable to asserting uncertain semantic names.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based function names such as `gm_801A5680`, `gm_801A57A8`, and `gm_801A5F64` while adding lifecycle comments instead of inventing symbol names.
Used `minor_data`, `vs_data`, `match_start_data`, `match_end`, and `exit_info` as descriptive local names for scene and match structures.

Documented known or suspected roles in trailing comments: `/// Returns KO Star Counts`, `/// Zeroes KO Star Counts`, `/// CSS_Enter`, `/// CSS_Exit`, `/// SSS_Enter`, `/// SSS_Exit`, `/// Match_Enter`, and tentative `??` labels for uncertain sudden-death/results functions.
Retained `fn_` prefixes for still-unknown local/static-style symbols and `gm_` prefixes for game-mode functions, matching existing project convention.
Used typed forward declarations rather than full includes where possible, adding `mn/forward.h` for `StartMeleeData`, `VsModeData`, and menu-related types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched several functions by replacing placeholder `/// #function` stubs with C implementations that preserve six-iteration player loops and exact scene-control branching.
Used array typing for `gm_804D6730[6]` to make KO star count pointer arithmetic and CSS storage match naturally.
Preserved explicit casts and integer constants visible in the diff, such as `0x14`, `0x18`, `0x80`, `0xC0`, `0x21`, `0x148`, and `0xFFFF`, which likely helped alignment with original control flow.
Used function pointer callback signatures in `gm_801A583C` and `gm_801A5C3C` to match optional transformation hooks for `StartMeleeData` and per-player `PlayerInitData` copies.
For team-mode loser selection, constructed a temporary `losers[6]` array combining player loser state with team loser state shifted by 8, then selected the smallest active-player value.

Matched audio setup by building a `u64 mask` through repeated ORs of `lbAudioAx_80026E84((u32)css_data->data.data.players[i].x0)` and then flushing through `lbAudioAx_80026F2C`, `lbAudioAx_8002702C`, and `lbAudioAx_80027168`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.

The diff itself contains an inline TODO in `gm_801A5F64`: `not sure how to match this, maybe gm_804D6730 is the wrong type?`, indicating unresolved uncertainty around updating KO star counts through `*gm_804D6730` inside a player loop.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching `gm_804D6730` to see whether the KO star count array type or update logic in `gm_801A5F64` was revised.
Search for later renames or comments that confirm the tentative `SuddenDeath_Enter`, `SuddenDeath_Exit`, `Results_Enter`, and `Results_Exit` labels in `gmvsmelee.h`.
Look for subsequent matches around `gm_80172DD4`, `gm_80172D78`, `gm_80172E74`, `gm_80172F00`, and `gm_801721EC` to clarify the unlock/minor-scene branch in `gm_801A5F64`.
Verify whether `SSSData::start_game` remains `u8` in later structure cleanup PRs.
Search later audio PRs for more precise names or semantics of `lbAudioAx_80026E84` and `lbAudioAx_80026EBC`.
